{"query":"Israeli settlement expansion West Bank 2025 land size location announcement","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1222,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.ohchr.org/en/press-releases/2026/03/israels-settlement-expansion-drives-mass-displacement-west-bank-un-report","title":"Israel’s settlement expansion drives mass displacement in West Bank – UN report | OHCHR","content":"Daily attacks by armed settlers, soldiers and “settler soldiers” – many of whom have been armed, equipped and trained by State authorities – along with the closure of more land to Palestinian landowners and harvesters made the 2025 season the worst in decades."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c1j5954edlno","title":"Israel announces major expansion of settlements in occupied West Bank","content":"Israeli ministers say 22 new Jewish settlements have been approved in the occupied West Bank - the biggest expansion in decades.
Others are completely new, according to Defence Minister Israel Katz and Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich.
The Israeli anti-settlement watchdog Peace Now called it "the most extensive move of its kind" in more than 30 years and warned that it would "dramatically reshape the West Bank and entrench the occupation even further".
However, expansion has risen sharply since Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu returned to power in late 2022 at the head of a right-wing, pro-settler coalition, as well as the start of the Gaza war, triggered by Hamas's 7 October 2023 attack on Israel.
Elisha Ben Kimon, an Israeli journalist with the popular Ynet news site who covers the West Bank and settlements, told the BBC's Newshour programme that 70% to 80% of ministers wanted to declare the formal annexation of the West Bank."},{"url":"https://www.crisisgroup.org/visual-explainers/israeli-settlements","title":"[Visual] Mapping Israeli Settlement Expansion (Updated 2025) | International Crisis Group","content":"Read our full report on Israeli West Bank annexation here."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/3/israel-approves-plan-to-establish-13-new-settlements-in-occupied-west-bank","title":"Israel approves plan to establish 13 new settlements in occupied West Bank | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera","content":"Israel’s Security Cabinet has approved a plan to establish 13 new settlements in the central occupied West Bank, a move Palestinian officials say will further fragment the territory and isolate East Jerusalem from its surrounding Palestinian communities.
Israel’s Channel 7 reported that the cabinet approved the construction of the illegal settlements on Thursday in the Binyamin regional area, one of the largest settlement blocs in the occupied West Bank.
“The plan seeks to create new geographical realities on the ground,” the governorate added in a statement, warning that the expansion would “undermine the prospects of establishing a geographically contiguous Palestinian state.”
The Binyamin plan follows reports that settlement movements are preparing to target Area A, territory under full Palestinian control, in what would constitute a violation of the Oslo Accords."},{"url":"https://www.cnn.com/2025/05/29/middleeast/israel-west-bank-settlements-expansion-intl","title":"Israel approves biggest expansion of West Bank settlements in decades | CNN","content":"World Middle East 4 min read
Nabil Abu Rudeineh, a spokesman for the president of the Palestinian Authority, called the announcement a “dangerous escalation and a challenge to international legitimacy and international law.”
In a statement issued earlier in the week after reports emerged of the settlement approval, Abu Rudeineh said the move would “perpetuate regional violence and instability.”
The United Kingdom’s minister for the Middle East and North Africa, Hamish Falconer, called the move a “deliberate obstacle to Palestinian statehood.”
“The Israeli government no longer pretends otherwise: the annexation of the Occupied Territories and expansion of settlements is its central goal.”"}]}

The Israeli newspaper Yediot Aharonot reports that Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich has earmarked $843million (NIS 2.7billion) over the next five years to expand settlements and outposts across the West Bank, describing it as de facto annexation. An IDF officers told a popular Israeli news program that the IDF is fully aware of and helping to facilitate the establishment of new illegal outposts, reporting that the head of the IDF Central Command Maj. Gen. Andalou Agency reports that on December 11th, the Israeli Security Cabinet approved plans to legalize 19 outposts, including the reestablishment of the Ganim and Kadim settlements in the northern West Bank (which were dismantled by the Israeli government in 2005 along with Homesh and Sa-Nur, both of which have already received government approval to be reestablished).","score":0.8145405,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZJeKVh769MQ","title":"Illegal settlement expansion: How Israel is redrawing occupied West Bank","content":"Illegal settlement expansion: How Israel is redrawing occupied West Bank

2025 has been an unprecedented year for Israeli settlement expansion in the occupied West Bank. 
Israel has approved 41 illegal settlements and in the past three years it has forcibly taken one million parcels of land in the occupied territory. 
 
Al Jazeera’s Nida Ibrahim reports from the occupied West Bank, Palestine.
